reasoning: Chegg faces severe structural headwinds from AI, but there is no acute catalyst to force a bankruptcy filing in the extremely short ~55-day window before July 1, 2025. The company ended Q3 2024 with over $600 million in cash and investments. Importantly, they have been using this liquidity to proactively repurchase their 2026 convertible notes, including a $56.6 million repurchase scheduled for March 2025. This behavior strongly signals that Chegg is not facing an imminent liquidity crunch. While they announced a "strategic review" in early 2025, such processes typically take longer than two months to play out, and their positive free cash flow provides a runway of at least 1–2 years. Without an immediate debt maturity or cash shortfall, a Chapter 11 filing before July 1 is highly unlikely.
